column1: AI Learning Coach — Full Test Pass
Thanks for testing! This is the full pass covering every area of the app across all three roles. For a quick 15-minute version, use the Smoke tab instead.

column2: The AI Learning Coach at https://learning-agent-pearl.vercel.app — our internal app that coaches employees on using AI in their own tools.
~45-60 min for a full pass. You can split it across sittings, or filter the Role column to just your role.
Filter the Role column: Learners run Learner + General rows, Managers add Manager rows, Admins add Admin rows. Use your real Housecall Pro email.
For each row: do the Action, compare to the Expected Result, then fill the last 3 columns.
Mark Pass (worked as expected), Fail (wrong/broken), or Blocked (couldn't get to this step). Use these exact words — the summary tab counts them.
Only if Fail/Blocked. High = blocks me or looks broken/embarrassing. Med = works but wrong/confusing. Low = cosmetic/nitpick.
Always welcome — especially what confused you, what you expected instead, and anything you loved. Screenshots help; note 'screenshot sent to Skylar'.
Don't stop — mark it Blocked/Fail, jot a note, and move on to the next row you can reach.
High = must-work core flow, Med = important, Low = nice-to-have. If you're short on time, do the High rows first.

column7: Tester Action
column8: Expected Result

column7: Open https://learning-agent-pearl.vercel.app in Chrome on a laptop. Note your browser and OS in the Notes column.
column8: App loads without errors; you land on the welcome / identity screen.

column7: Record which role you are testing as (Learner / Manager / Admin) so we know which sections to expect.
column8: N/A — informational.

column7: On a fresh browser (or after clearing site data), open the app.
column8: You see 'Welcome to AI Learning Coach' with an email field.

column7: Enter your real Housecall Pro email (name@housecallpro.com) and continue.
column8: You are let into the app; no error. Your name/email is recognized on later screens.

column7: Try entering a clearly invalid email (e.g. 'abc') and continue.
column8: App rejects it / prompts for a valid email rather than letting you through.

column7: As a first-time user, proceed through onboarding and enter your name.
column8: Name field accepts input and carries through to the rest of onboarding.

column7: Select your role/team from the options. If yours isn't listed, choose 'Something else not listed here'.
column8: Selection is saved; the app says it will tailor your learning path to your team.

column7: On the tool step, set your Primary tool (e.g. Gemini, ChatGPT, Copilot).
column8: Tool is saved as your primary tool.

column7: Add a second tool using 'Add another tool (e.g. Perplexity)'.
column8: Second tool is added and shown in your tool list.

column7: Choose a first goal/topic you want to learn.
column8: Goal is accepted; you are routed into the app (home or a tailored lesson/path).

column7: Open Calibrate (sidebar: Your Progress > Calibrate) and start the Skill Calibration.
column8: Calibration scenarios/questions load one at a time.

column7: Answer all calibration questions to completion.
column8: You see a 'Calibrated' state / result, and your skill levels update.

column7: Navigate to Home (logo or Home).
column8: You see a personalized greeting (Good morning/afternoon/evening + your name) and your tier/level badge.

column7: On Home, find the 'Today's Pick' card and click it.
column8: A relevant lesson/activity opens that matches your role/goals.

column7: On Home, use a Quick Action button (e.g. start a lesson, open a game).
column8: The action routes you to the correct destination.

column7: On Home, locate the streak card and XP/stats pills.
column8: Streak count and XP totals display and reflect your activity (not blank/zero after completing something).

column7: On Home, view the leaderboard widget.
column8: Leaderboard renders with names/scores; your position is visible.

column7: Open Discovery (sidebar: Learn > Discovery).
column8: A discovery view loads with AI use cases relevant to your role.

column7: Pick a use case that interests you and open it.
column8: Detail/lesson opens and is relevant; no broken content.

column7: Open Library (sidebar: Learn > Library).
column8: Categories display as collapsible groups with colored pills.

column7: Expand and collapse several categories.
column8: Categories expand/collapse smoothly and show use cases inside.

column7: From Library, open a specific use case.
column8: Use case content loads correctly and is readable.

column7: Go to Lesson (or generate from a topic). Enter a specific topic (e.g. 'Writing follow-up emails to customers with AI').
column8: A lesson is generated on that exact topic with no clarify prompt.

column7: Read through the generated lesson slides.
column8: Content is coherent, on-topic, and free of obvious errors or placeholder text.

column7: Generate a lesson from a vague topic (e.g. 'AI' or 'help me').
column8: The app asks a clarifying question instead of generating a generic lesson.

column7: Answer the clarifying prompt with specifics.
column8: A focused lesson is then generated matching your clarification.

column7: In a lesson with narration, press Play on the audio.
column8: Narration plays clearly and is in sync with the current slide.

column7: Advance to the next slide while audio plays.
column8: Audio transitions appropriately to the new slide; no overlap of two narrations.

column7: Start a lesson, then leave it midway (navigate away).
column8: The lesson is saved as paused (paused-lessons bell shows a count).

column7: Open the paused-lessons bell and resume the lesson.
column8: You return to the same lesson at (or near) where you left off.

column7: Inside a lesson, use the refine/regenerate option to make it simpler or more specific.
column8: Lesson regenerates reflecting your refinement; old content is replaced.

column7: Complete a lesson that ends with a quiz/interactive activity.
column8: Quiz works (can select answers, gets scored).

column7: Finish the lesson fully.
column8: A completion state shows and you receive XP (XP toast/popup appears).

column7: Open a lesson that includes video and play it.
column8: Video loads and plays with working controls; no broken embed.

column7: Open Just Chat (sidebar: Learn > Just Chat) and ask a general AI question.
column8: You get a helpful coaching response; it is distinct from the lesson-generation flow.

column7: Open Prompts (sidebar: Learn > Prompts) and browse prompts.
column8: Prompts list loads, organized/labeled clearly.

column7: Copy a prompt using its copy control.
column8: Prompt copies to clipboard and a confirmation appears.

column7: Open Today's Challenge (sidebar: Learn > Today's).
column8: A daily challenge loads for today's date.

column7: Complete the daily challenge.
column8: Completion is recorded and XP is awarded (once for the day).

column7: Open Games (sidebar: Learn > Games) and pick a game (Prompt Battle / Hallucination Hunt / Speed Round / AI or Human?).
column8: Game opens to a start screen with a minimized 'How to play' you can expand.

column7: Start and play a full round of the chosen game.
column8: Game is playable end-to-end; a score is shown at the end.

column7: Observe XP after finishing.
column8: XP is awarded based on difficulty × score; XP toast/popup appears.

column7: Replay the same game again on the same day.
column8: You can replay, but no additional XP is granted for the repeat (per content-day rule).

column7: Check the leaderboard after playing.
column8: Your game performance is reflected in your XP/standing.

column7: In a lesson, use the 'open tool' / LLM window callout link for your preferred tool.
column8: Your preferred AI tool opens in a new tab (e.g. Gemini), with guidance to use it.

column7: Go to My Tools / Profile and change your preferred AI tool.
column8: Selection saves and subsequent 'open tool' links point to the new tool.

column7: Open Achievements (sidebar: Your Progress > Achievements).
column8: Earned and locked achievements display; earned ones reflect your activity.

column7: Open Knowledge Heatmap (sidebar: Your Progress > Knowledge Heatmap).
column8: Heatmap renders your skill areas with strength shading.

column7: Open Leaderboard (sidebar: Your Progress > Leaderboard).
column8: Full leaderboard loads with rankings; your row is identifiable.

column7: Toggle the theme between light and dark.
column8: Entire UI switches cleanly; text stays readable, no broken/white patches.

column7: Enable a color-blindness / accessibility mode if available in settings.
column8: Colors adjust accordingly and the app remains usable.

column7: Start the Tour (sidebar: Settings > Tour).
column8: An interactive click-through tour spotlights real sections and lets you navigate.

column7: Complete or exit the tour.
column8: Tour ends gracefully and returns you to normal use.

column7: Open the Help widget, then navigate to another page.
column8: Help state behaves as expected (persists/closes as designed) without errors.

column7: Log out of the app.
column8: You are returned to the welcome/identity screen and the session is cleared.

column7: Log back in with the same email.
column8: Your profile, XP, and progress are still intact (not reset to a new account / no fresh welcome bonus re-granted).

column7: Open Team Dashboard (sidebar: Manager > Team Dashboard).
column8: Dashboard loads showing your team's learning activity/metrics.

column7: Review the team metrics for accuracy/sensibility.
column8: Numbers look reasonable (no obviously broken/empty/NaN values).

column7: Open Reporting (sidebar: Manager > Reporting).
column8: Reporting view loads with charts/tables that render correctly.

column7: Open Admin Dashboard (sidebar: Settings > Admin Dashboard).
column8: Admin landing shows sections: People & XP, Activity Log, Lesson QA, Lesson Content Preview, Menu Visibility, Skill Levels, AI Tools, Avatar Catalog, XP & Levels, Notifications, Admins.

column7: Open Menu Visibility, turn OFF one sidebar item (e.g. Games), and save.
column8: Setting saves without error.

column7: As a non-admin user (or via preview), check that item.
column8: The item now shows 'Coming soon' for regular users while admins still see it.

column7: Use the 'view as regular user' preview option.
column8: You see the app as a non-admin would, respecting menu-visibility toggles.

column7: Open Lesson QA and Lesson Content Preview.
column8: You can preview lesson content and QA tooling loads correctly.

column7: Open XP & Levels and Skill Levels.
column8: Current XP rules and skill-level definitions display; edits (if made) save.

column7: Open Activity Log.
column8: Recent user/learning activity entries are listed.

column7: Open the app on a phone (or narrow the browser window).
column8: Layout adapts; navigation is reachable; nothing is cut off or overlapping.

column7: Throughout testing, note any error messages, broken images, slow loads (>5s), or confusing wording.
column8: Capture each issue with the page, what you did, and what you expected in Notes.

column7: After finishing, rate how intuitive the app was (1-5) and note the single most confusing thing.
column8: Subjective feedback recorded for the team.
